steps.title: Identify the key prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that in low-visibility conditions, wider-headed fish will bump into obstructions more often than narrower-headed fish (because they detect vortices worse).

steps.title: Apply to the hypothesis
steps.content:
C directly compares a wide-headed fish to a narrow-headed fish, showing the wide-headed fish bumped into more obstructions in low-visibility conditions with medium vortices.

The other three either don't compare head types directly or show no difference between them.

This direct comparison showing wider-headed fish performing worse in low-visibility is what the hypothesis predicts.

C is correct.
